Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Lorentzen Co.
Off Calendar Jun-28-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Asbestos Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Judge: Gail Dekreon; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reported by Giselle Casey, CSR#8098. (514)

James Ghilotti, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, John M. Silva.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Lorentzen Co.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:
IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant Lorentzen Co., in the amount of $745,290.00 in Economic Damages, $2,120,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, minus $2,294,496.00 (80.079% attributable to TOSCO CORPORATION and Plaintiff per Jury Verdict) for a total amount of $570,794.00.
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, MAINTAINING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE.

Appearances:

Andrew Chew of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and moving party; Ryan C. Kujawski of Berkes Crane Robinson & Seal LLP (213.955.1150) for defendant Sequoia Ventures Inc.; Canon T. Young of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer (510.658.3600) appearing via Court Call for defendant Dillingham Construction, NA, Inc.; Emily E. Anselmo of Walsworth (415.781.7072) for defendants Thomas Dee Enginerering Co., CBS Corp., Genereal Electric Company.

Discussed and the Court modifies its ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for order granting preference in setting case for trial, maintaining the current trial date is granted. Trial date remains June 13, 2017, with preference status pursuant to C.C.P. 36(e). This action is assigned a return holding date of October 2, 2017 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 11, 2017. All pre-trial dates and deadlines remain dictated by the trial date. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by September 25, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on September 28, 2017. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on October 2, 2017 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by September 28, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Order signed. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR 7768 (Tel: 415.298.1538) (503/CML)
